---
title: "Career Coaching and Professional Advising"
description: "Mentoring and advising students and alumni as they connect academic experiences, geospatial skills, internships, capstone projects, and career goals."
date: 2024-01-01
categories: [mentoring, advising, career coaching, GEOG 499, student success, internships, professional development, alumni]
image: ../../assets/images/thumbnails/career-coaching-professional-advising.webp
---

The **Career Coaching and Professional Advising** page highlights my work helping students and alumni connect coursework, projects, internships, research, and geospatial skills to future opportunities.

This work includes one-on-one advising, GEOG 499 capstone mentoring, reference letters, internship and job opportunity sharing, alumni connections, career event follow-up, and professional pathway coaching.

## Advising Context

Students often need help understanding how their interests and skills translate into professional opportunities. A student may know they enjoy GIS, sustainability, geography, mapping, drones, planning, environmental work, or community projects, but not yet know what career paths connect to those interests.

My advising work helps students identify possible directions, understand the value of their skills, select meaningful experiences, and prepare to communicate their work to employers, graduate programs, and professional mentors.

## One-on-One Career Coaching

I regularly meet with students and alumni to discuss academic pathways, internships, jobs, graduate school, portfolios, resumes, project experience, and professional goals.

This support includes:

- helping students identify relevant career pathways,
- reviewing how course projects can become portfolio examples,
- discussing internships and job opportunities,
- connecting students with alumni and employers,
- helping students prepare for networking conversations,
- writing reference letters,
- supporting graduate school and fellowship applications,
- sharing opportunities through student communication channels.

## Professional Pathway Development

Career coaching also includes helping students understand the professional landscape around GIS, geography, sustainability, planning, UAS, and environmental data.

This work connects to broader department and program efforts, including:

- the Geography and Sustainability Career Mingle,
- alumni engagement,
- internship and job opportunity sharing,
- informal alumni networking,
- conference participation,
- TNGIC student engagement,
- professional organization involvement,
- career-focused class visits and advising conversations.

## Career Coaching Beyond the Department

This advising work also extends to community and partner groups when appropriate. For example, I hosted a four-part career coaching program for local AmeriCorps members affected by changes in federal policy and funding.

That work reflects a broader commitment to helping people translate their experiences, skills, and service into future professional opportunities.

## Mentoring Approach

My approach to career coaching is practical and student-centered. I try to help students see the value in the work they have already done, identify the experiences they still need, and communicate their strengths clearly.

This often means helping students answer questions such as:

- What kinds of jobs use these skills?
- Which projects should I include in a portfolio?
- How do I explain this GIS project to a non-GIS employer?
- What experiences would make me more competitive?
- Should I consider graduate school?
- How do I talk to alumni or employers?
- How do I turn a class project into a professional example?

## Mentoring Significance

Career coaching and professional advising help students connect academic experiences to life after graduation. This work is especially important in applied fields like GIS, where students often graduate with valuable technical skills but need help explaining those skills in professional language.

By supporting students through advising conversations, capstone mentoring, references, alumni connections, and opportunity sharing, I help students build confidence, make informed decisions, and move toward meaningful next steps.
